recent_coach_change: No coaching change in the last 12 months; Hong Myung-bo led the team through AFC qualifying and named the World Cup squad.
qualification_path: Qualified directly via AFC third round, topping Group C with five wins and one draw (16 points, unbeaten) ahead of nations including China, Thailand and Singapore; an 11th consecutive World Cup appearance.
key_players: Son Heung-min (LAFC) — captain, gearing up for his fourth World Cup at age 33
Lee Kang-in (Paris Saint-Germain) — creative midfielder; limited starts at PSG but top assist provider in qualifying (5 goals, 6 assists in 15 games)
Kim Min-jae (Bayern Munich) — center-back; third choice at club but a defensive leader who dominated aerial duels in qualifying

injuries_doubts.issue: Withdrew injured from the squad; replaced by Cho Wi-je

squad_notes: Final squad revealed on 16 May 2026. Son Heung-min named captain for his fourth World Cup. Cho Wi-je was called up on 2 June 2026 as an injury replacement for Cho Yu-min. No injury concerns reported for Son himself.
warmup_assessment: Mixed but ultimately positive: South Korea earned a narrow 1-0 win over El Salvador in their final World Cup warm-up on 4 June 2026 in Provo, Utah, decided by a Lee Dong-gyeong free-kick. Hong acknowledged early offensive difficulties but called the match useful preparation.
shape_verdict: South Korea arrive in solid shape after dominant, unbeaten AFC qualifying and a winning final warm-up, anchored by captain Son Heung-min and a core of European-based players, though they were not fully fluent offensively in the El Salvador friendly.

news.headline: South Korea edge El Salvador 1-0 in final World Cup warm-up via Lee Dong-gyeong free-kick
Cho Wi-je called up as injury replacement for Cho Yu-min in South Korea's World Cup squad
Hong Myung-bo names South Korea World Cup squad; Son Heung-min set for fourth World Cup as captain
